root_text.an8.87:1.1: “Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pattaṁ nikkujjeyya.
root_text.an8.87:1.3: Bhikkhūnaṁ alābhāya parisakkati, bhikkhūnaṁ anatthāya parisakkati, bhikkhūnaṁ avāsāya parisakkati, bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati, bhikkhū bhikkhūhi bhedeti, buddhassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, dhammassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, saṅghassa avaṇṇaṁ bhāsati.
root_text.an8.87:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahaṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pattaṁ nikkujjeyya.
root_text.an8.87:2.1: Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pattaṁ ukkujjeyya.
root_text.an8.87:2.3: Na bhikkhūnaṁ alābhāya parisakkati, na bhikkhūnaṁ anatthāya parisakkati, na bhikkhūnaṁ avāsāya parisakkati, na bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati, na bhikkhū bhikkhūhi bhedeti, buddhassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, dhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, saṅghassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati.
root_text.an8.87:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahaṅgehi samannāgatassa upāsakassa ākaṅkhamāno saṅgho pattaṁ ukkujjeyyā”ti.

translation_text.an8.87:1.1: “Mendicants, the Saṅgha may, if it wishes, turn the bowl upside down for a lay follower on eight grounds.
translation_text.an8.87:1.3: They try to prevent the mendicants from getting material things. They try to harm mendicants. They try to drive mendicants from a monastery. They insult and abuse mendicants. They divide mendicants against each other. They criticize the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an8.87:1.4: The Saṅgha may, if it wishes, turn the bowl upside down for a lay follower on these eight grounds.
translation_text.an8.87:2.1: The Saṅgha may, if it wishes, turn the bowl upright for a lay follower on eight grounds.
translation_text.an8.87:2.3: They don’t try to prevent the mendicants from getting material things. They don’t try to harm mendicants. They don’t try to drive mendicants from a monastery. They don’t insult and abuse mendicants. They don’t divide mendicants against each other. They don’t criticize the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an8.87:2.4: The Saṅgha may, if it wishes, turn the bowl upright for a lay follower on these eight grounds.”

variant_text.an8.87:1.3: avāsāya → anāvāsāya (si, sya-all, pts1ed) | bhedeti → vibhedeti (sya-all, pts1ed)

reference_text.an8.87:1.1: csp1ed17.158, csp2ed17.158, dr23.373, ms17A8_840, msdiv87, pts-vp-pli4.345
